title: `--network pasta:--map-gw` example in documentation does not seem to be a valid current passt option
body:
### Issue Description

`--map-gw` is listed as an example pasta configuration in [network.md](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/source/markdown/options/network.md). This does not seem to currently exist in the latest passt version, and mapping the host to the guest's gateway address appears to be the current default, with `--no-map-gw` available to disable this behaviour. Relevant options for `--map-gw` adjacent behaviour are `--map-host-loopback` and `--map-guest-addr` but thats already mentioned in the docs.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `man podman-network-create`
2. Scroll to `--network` option documentation
3. compare with `man pasta`


### Describe the results you received

`--map-gw` is listed in the Podman docs but not in the Passt docs.

### Describe the results you expected

`--map-gw` not listed in the Podman docs.

### podman info output

comments.body:
See podman-run(1) (emphasis mine):

> [...] `--no-map-gw is also assumed by default, to avoid direct access from container to host using the gateway address. The latter can be overridden by passing --map-gw in the pasta-specific options (` **`despite not being an actual pasta(1) option`**`).`

`--map-gw` is an option we introduced in Podman because Podman configures pasta with `--no-map-gw` by default, and we need a way to disable that from Podman. The gateway mapping function is practical in stand-alone pasta, but undesired in Podman.

Does that clarify things for you? Would you consider submitting a change to the documentation if the current version doesn't provide enough clarity?
Ah, nah, I think I just can't read very well (or at least at 6am). Closing this slightly embarassing issue, sorry !
